arts.ttl: Manchester City v Arsenal: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Match at the Etihad Stadium kicks off at 4.30pm (BST) Man City’s bluntness against Inter may give Arteta ideas Share your thoughts with Rob via email You can usually judge the health of a league by its biggest rivalry. Think AC Milan v Napoli in the late 1980s, Man Utd v Arsenal at the turn of the century and Barcelona v Real Madrid when Pep and Jose were in charge. Manchester City v Arsenal isn’t quite in that category, probably never will be, but it would make a worthy Champions League final and that’s a very good starting point for any football-based ding-dong. This is season three of a rivalry that began when Arsenal knocked Liverpool off the perch reserved for City’s biggest challengers. City gave Arsenal a brutal introduction to life at the highest level, belting them home and away in 2022-23, but last season the only City player to score against Arsenal was Cole Palmer in the Community Shield. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arsenal utility man attracting Serie A transfer interest
arts.sum: Inter Milan and Napoli are among the clubs courting Arsenal defender Takehiro Tomiyasu. Tomiyasu has been linked with leaving Arsenal after struggling to cement a regular role in Mikel Arteta’s plans, with the Japan international starting just 10 league games in 2023/24. A knee injury has delayed the defender’s start to the current campaign […] The post Arsenal utility man attracting Serie A transfer interest first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola vs Mikel Arteta – The head-to-head record
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola and Mikel Arteta pit their wits against each other once again this weekend, as Manchester City take on Arsenal in the Premier League. Friends who have become rivals, Arteta began his coaching career on Guardiola’s staff at Manchester City before leaving to take charge of Arsenal in December 2019. In recent seasons, Arteta’s […] The post Pep Guardiola vs Mikel Arteta – The head-to-head record first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Man City and Arsenal combined XI: The Gunners' centre back partnership stands strong and there's a place for both Rodri and Declan Rice - but who gets the nod behind Erling Haaland?
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola is still without long-term absentee Oscar Bobb, while Nathan Ake suffered a thigh injury while on international duty and Kevin De Bruyne remains a doubt.

arts.ttl: £25m Arsenal transfer target breaks silence and says 'I had to be calm'
arts.sum: Arsenal made Raya a permanent addition following his loan spell at the Emirates Stadium last season. Joan Garcia has opened up on the intense transfer speculation he was subject to this summer.

arts.ttl: Raheem Sterling and Riccardo Calafiori drop double Arsenal team news hint before Man City clash
arts.sum: Raheem Sterling and Riccardo Calafiori have both dropped hints on Arsenal''s starting line-up ahead of their early season title showdown with Manchester City. Both Calafiori and Sterling were introduced in the second-half against Atalanta, while Thomas Partey - who also posted on his social media ahead of Sunday's clash - started.

arts.ttl: How to watch Man City v Arsenal - TV Channel, live stream details, kick off time
arts.sum: Arsenal travel to the North West on Sunday as they face Premier League champions Manchester City. Sunday's trip to the Etihad Stadium is an early season title showdown in the Premier League with Arsenal capable of going top with a win.

arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka hits back at Rodri before Man City vs Arsenal clash
arts.sum: Going for the title with Manchester City last season, the Gunners earned a share of the spoils at the home of the Sky Blues after beating Pep Guardiola's side in the reverse fixture in North London. We went to Manchester City to win and we are going to do that again this time.
